titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 570

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=882 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.392093+00 | translation moa=883 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:12:05.403032+00

p.cl:
. DD . dei gratia Rex Scottorum . Omnibus episcopis suis . et Comitibus . et Baronibus . Simulque omnibus
probis hominibus suis totius terre sue . salutem .

Scitote me dedisse et concessisse deo et sancto Chvthberto
et monachis eiusdem sancti . Ecclesiam Sanctę Marię de Berewic . cum terra . et decimis . et omnibus
rectitdinibus predicte ecclesie . terra . et aqua pertinentibus . et hoc in excambio pro ecclesia de
Malros . et pro rectitudinibus quas ibi habuerunt . Ita libere . et quiete . et honorifice . sicut
umquam liberius . et quietius . et honorabilius . tenuerunt ecclesiam . et Res suas de Melros . Concedente . et
confirmante hoc Henrico filio meo .

Testibus Iohanne episcopo . et Herberto Abbate de Rochesburc .
et Willelmo nepote Regis . et Acelino archidiacono . et Roberto de humframwilla . et estmundo clerico
et Berenger hengaine . et Gualeran capellano . et Roberto Grimbal . et Normano vicecomite . et Willelmo
de sumerewilla . et Roberto de Burnovilla .
David by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all his bishops, earls, barons, as well as all worthy men of his entire land, greeting.

Know me to have given and conceded to God, Saint Cuthbert, and the monks of the same saint, the church of St Mary at Berwick, with land, teinds, and all the revenues pertaining to the aforesaid church on land and water, and this in exchange for the church of Melrose and for the revenues which they had there, so freely, quitly, and honourably as they ever most freely, quitly and honourably held their church and property at Melrose. Henry my son allows and affirms this.

As witnesses: John the bishop, Herbert the abbot of Roxburgh, William the king's nephew, Ascelin the archdeacon, Robert d'Umfraville, Eastmund the clerk, Berengar Engaine, Waleran the chaplain, Robert Grimbal, Norman the sheriff, William de Sumerville, and Robert de Bournville.
